data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: UNH, MELI, CELH, XYZ, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Friday as investors digested the rise in initial jobless claims, signaling a modest softening in the labor market. Investors eagerly awaited the consumer sentiment report for further insights into economic conditions and consumer confidence.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) shares surged 12% after delivering strong Q4 results that exceeded expectations across key metrics, including active buyers, gross merchandise volume (GMV), and payment volume, driving a 37.4% Y/Y increase in total sales. Revenue of $6.1B beat estimates by $120M, fueled by a 33% rise in total payment volume and an 8% increase in GMV. EPS soared to $12.61, nearly quadrupling from the prior year. Brazil, its largest market, saw a 13% GMV increase and a 38% jump in consolidated revenue and financial income.
  • Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH) jumped 18% after confirming its $1.8B acquisition of rival energy drink brand Alani Nu alongside strong Q4 earnings. The deal, expected to close in Q2, includes $1.65B in price and $150M in tax assets, with $900M in debt financing and $375M in cash on hand. Celsius expects the acquisition to drive topline growth, be cash EPS accretive in year one, and strengthen its position in the global energy drink market, which is projected to grow at a 10% CAGR through 2029. The company also anticipates $50M in run-rate cost synergies over two years.

Biggest stock losers

  • Block (NYSE:XYZ) shares tumbled nearly 7% after missing Q4 earnings estimates and issuing a disappointing outlook for Q1 and FY2025. The company guided Q1 gross profit at $2.32B, below the $2.38B Visible Alpha consensus, and expects adjusted operating income of $430M, up from $402M in Q4 2024. For FY2025, Block forecasted gross profit of $10.22B, trailing the $10.27B consensus but rising from $8.89B in 2024, with expectations for Cash App and Square to improve in the latter half of the year. It reaffirmed its goal to achieve Rule of 40 in 2026 and exit 2025 at or above that threshold on a quarterly basis. CEO Jack Dorsey highlighted plans to integrate its ecosystems, expand sales efforts, and invest in AI tools, while noting increased marketing spending in H2 2024 yielded strong returns.
  • IAMGOLD (NYSE:IAG) shares plunged as much as 9% after falling short of expectations in Q4 results, despite a ~58% Y/Y revenue increase. Looking ahead, the company expects total attributable production in 2025 between 735,000 and 820,000 ounces, up from 2024, driven by Côté Gold’s ramp-up and steady output from Essakane and Westwood. Côté Gold’s first full-year production is projected at 360,000–400,000 ounces (250,000–280,000 ounces attributable). Cash costs are forecasted at $1,200–$1,350 per ounce, slightly higher due to increased costs at Essakane. Capital expenditures are set at $310M, with $290M allocated for sustaining capital.
  • Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU) shares slumped 9% after reporting Q4 earnings and revenue that missed estimates, as purchase volume declined sequentially to $31.1B from $32.6B in Q3 but rose from $23.3B in Q4 2023. The Brazilian digital bank added 5.5M new customers, bringing its total to 99.3M. Monthly average revenue per active customer increased to $11.4 from $10.6 in Q3 and $8.6 a year ago, while the cost to serve per customer remained steady at $0.9, up from $0.8 in Q4 2023.
  • Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) plummeted 9% despite strong Q4 results, as its 2025 guidance fell short of expectations. The company projected adjusted EPS of $6.00–$6.40, missing the $6.82 consensus, and revenue of $4.0B–$4.2B, with a midpoint of $4.1B below the $4.25B consensus. It also forecast an adjusted operating margin of 28%, trailing expectations. For Q1, Akamai expects adjusted EPS of $1.54–$1.59, below the $1.61 consensus, and revenue of $1.0B–$1.02B, missing the $1.04B consensus.
  • UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) shares lost ~11% following a report that the U.S. Department of Justice ((DoJ)) has launched a civil fraud investigation into the company’s business practices. The probe, which also reportedly involves the Office of Inspector General at the Department of Health and Human Services, focuses on allegations that UnitedHealth inflated patient diagnoses to secure higher Medicare Advantage payments. This adds to the company’s ongoing regulatory challenges, including a DoJ antitrust investigation in February and a lawsuit in November to block its $3.3B acquisition of Amedisys (AMED), raising concerns over its business operations and future growth.

data.title: Trump Media board OKs exploration of funding options for M&A, partnerships
data.description:
Truth Social parent Trump Media (NASDAQ:DJT) said Friday the company's board has voted to authorize the creation of a strategic acquisition fund with select investors, which will help them with financing strategies for possible M&A activity.

"TMTG is exploring opportunities to partner, merge with, and/or acquire other participants in the growing America First Economy that would benefit from the Corporation’s technology and branding—and that are able to function effectively if the Corporation evolves into a holding company with numerous, largely autonomous subsidiaries in a variety of industries," the company said in a statement.

"The America First economy is a fantastic sector with enormous potential, and we want all its participants to know that TMTG intends to make this market even greater," CEO Devin Nunes said.

In January, President Donald Trump's company launched its financial services and FinTech brand, Truth.Fi. The DJT board approved an investment of up to $250M to be custodied by Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) as part of the launch.

The funds may be allocated to customized SMAs, customized ETFs, bitcoin, and similar cryptocurrencies or crypto-related securities, in addition to traditional investment vehicles, the company said.

data.title: Dow, S&P, and Nasdaq are mixed as DoJ's probe on UnitedHealth weighs on markets
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were mixed on Friday, as markets look to regain some momentum after Thursday's sell-off, which was partly driven by Walmart's disappointing outlook.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.1%, the Nasdaq Compisite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.6%.

The Dow tumbled, in part driven by UnitedHealth's (UNH) 9% drop, after The Wall Street Journal reported that the U.S. Department of Justice has launched a new investigation into the company’s business practices.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was lower by 2 basis points to 4.49%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was unchanged at 4.27%.

U.S. stocks finished with losses Thursday, with the S&P 500 pulling back from a record high, in part as investors balked at Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

"We saw a moderate risk-off move yesterday, with the S&P 500 falling back from its all-time high," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The main story in the U.S. was a weaker-than-expected forecast from Walmart, which added to nerves about the health of the consumer right now, especially after a soft retail sales print last week, Reid added.

On the economic side, the February PMI composite flash and January existing home sales are due shortly. The latter is expected to come in at 4.13M.

The February consumer sentiment is forecasted to come in at 67.8.

"Final February U.S. Michigan consumer sentiment data is mainly of use as a source of entertainment, such is the degree of political partisanship," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

However, approval for U.S. President Donald Trump’s economic policies and Trump mega-donor Musk has been falling, and the Michigan sentiment may hint at whether that disapproval is partisan or not, Donovan added.

data.title: Spirit Airlines confirms agreement to emerge from bankruptcy
data.description:
• Spirit Airlines (OTC:SAVEQ) confirmed that a federal court has approved its reorganization plan and will emerge from bankruptcy protection in the coming weeks.
  • Under the approved plan, Spirit will eliminate $795M of its debt, receive $350M in new equity investment, and issue $840M new senior secured debt to existing bondholders.
  • Spirit (OTC:SAVEQ) will also enter in a new revolving credit facility of up to $300M.
  • Under the terms of this agreement, vendors, aircraft lessors, and aircraft bondholders will not be impaired.
  • The company will then be owned by its majority bondholders.
  • Shares opened another 7% lower while the remaining airline sector is trading with gains of 1%-2%.
  • Press Release

data.title: Vanguard says don’t overlook value stocks in the AI boom
data.description:
Vanguard, one of the world’s largest asset managers, emphasized that investors seeking to capitalize on the rapid growth of artificial intelligence must not overlook value stocks as a key avenue for exposure to this transformative technology.

“Conventional wisdom may lead one to think that growth stocks are the way investors can position themselves to take advantage of the growth of artificial intelligence,” Vanguard’s global chief economist Joe Davis said. Davis also added that “investors would be wise not to overlook value stocks.”

If AI proves to be a groundbreaking technology, Vanguard’s analysis suggests that the U.S. will experience the most significant long-term gains in productivity and growth. Moreover, should AI truly transform industries, its influence is expected to extend beyond just the tech sector.

On the other hand, if AI fails to meet expectations, growth stocks could face notable losses, while value stocks may have a better chance of outperforming. Given the current high valuations, “investing solely in tech stocks right now appears risky due to their current valuations,” Davis added.

Outlined below are a group of artificial intelligence and value focused exchange traded funds that investors can use as proxy investments.

AI ETFs: (NASDAQ:AIQ), (NASDAQ:BOTZ), (NYSEARCA:DTEC), (NYSEARCA:GINN), (NASDAQ:ROBT), (NYSEARCA:TECB), (NASDAQ:XT), (NYSEARCA:THNQ), and (NYSEARCA:CHAT).

Value ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VTV), (NYSEARCA:VBR), (BATS:EFV), (NYSEARCA:AVUV), (NYSEARCA:CGDV), (NYSEARCA:DFUV), (NYSEARCA:DFAT), (NYSEARCA:DFIV), and (NYSEARCA:IJJ).
